cards.title: Proposal & Declaration
cards.content:
ΓÇó Proposal Form: The primary document based on which underwriting is done.
ΓÇó Medical Questionnaire: Detailed history of diseases or past surgeries.
ΓÇó Declaration: A formal statement that all information provided is completely true.

cards.title: Section 64VB & Premium
cards.content:
ΓÇó Section 64VB: "No Premium, No Cover". Risk is NOT assumed until premium is received in advance.
ΓÇó Grace Period: Extra time given to renew a policy (usually 30 days for health) without losing continuity benefits.

items.text: Conditions that must be strictly complied with. Breach leads to policy cancellation.
Any post-issuance changes (e.g., adding a new family member) are done via endorsements.
If a policy condition is ambiguous, the interpretation favoring the INSURED is applied.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: The Agent's Duty is to ensure the prospect fills the proposal form accurately and discloses all material facts.
KYC (Know Your Customer) and AML (Anti-Money Laundering) documents are mandatory for all proposals.
Prospectus: A mandatory document containing details of the product, coverage, and exclusions.
Renewal Notice: Although insurers usually send a notice 30 days prior, it is the sole responsibility of the INSURED to renew on time.
Pre-existing Disease (PED): Any condition diagnosed or treated up to 48 months (standard) prior to the policy start date.
